title: betula glandulosa var. glandulifera
intro: Betula glandulosa var. glandulifera, also known as Korean silver birch, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive white bark and small, yellow-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to the presence of salicin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Betula glandulosa var. glandulifera include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and extract, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
